mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment rises gradually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and retail sales indicates synchronized strength across production and consumer spending, generating robust corporate revenue growth that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series decouples, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 55% over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and near-term economic momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inventory correction phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 250 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns when labor market tightness triggers aggressive monetary tightening, or directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and credit availability, which stimulates upstream industrial activity and financial sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts rise while equity markets decline due to interest rate sensitivity, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when declines are driven by seasonal adjustments or temporary supply chain disruptions, or accuracy drops below 55%.
